title: GAME GoS Special Edition and GoS 2 released on PSP
display_title: GAME GoS Special Edition and GoS 2 released on PSP
wikitext:
[]: [GAME] GoS Special Edition and GoS 2 released on PSP!             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Feb 13, 2009 7:52 pm Looks like Gainax beat Zeak to it: http://www.evageeks.org/2009/02/girlfriend-of-steel-to-be-released-on-sony-psp/ Discuss. Personally, I'm glad. Now I can see the changes between the Special edition compared to the regular version! (yay for having a PSP!)

[]:             Quote Post by Great Genius Shinji-Sama » Fri Feb 13, 2009 8:25 pm in Japan or us, SSD? If its in us Im happy cuz I can get it easily then. But if its only available in Japan im indifferent. Cause I wont be able to play it. But YAY for Mana! I her.

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Fri Feb 13, 2009 8:34 pm Coolio, I will probably get the limited edition as I have the other PSP box set.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Feb 13, 2009 9:14 pm PSP games are region-free, meaning they play in any PSP unit if you import the game. PSP videos however play only for the region your unit is from.

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Fri Feb 13, 2009 11:31 pm I just sold my PSP a while ago but that too bad for me. I always wanted to play the orignal GOS before though but I will end up importing the Sega Saturn game in the future soon.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Feb 14, 2009 9:37 am The original post from Gainax: http://www.gainax.co.jp/anime/eva/news/0198.html Much more useful than Amazon.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Feb 14, 2009 11:24 am Thanks. In my state of illness, I completely forgot about checking there.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Feb 14, 2009 12:59 pm I'm surprised they're still porting a game that's over 10 years old. I mean, does anyone really care about this game anymore?

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Sat Feb 14, 2009 1:04 pm If games like Final Fantasy 4 cameout on GBA, see why not. Fans who never play the game on the Saturn would like to play it now.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Feb 14, 2009 2:03 pm If the cow can still yield more milk...

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Feb 14, 2009 2:14 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: I'm surprised they're still porting a game that's over 10 years old.

[]:             Quote Post by Holy Diver » Sat Feb 14, 2009 2:57 pm Mr. Tines wrote: If the cow can still yield more milk...

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Feb 14, 2009 3:46 pm Guyver Spawn wrote: Fans who never play the game on the Saturn, or PlayStation, or Windows, or Macintosh, or PlayStation 2, or Windows (again) would like to play it now on a system that no one really cares about.

[]:             Quote Post by Great Genius Shinji-Sama » Sat Feb 14, 2009 8:58 pm what would be the best way of ordering/purchassing the game - GAINAX or Amazon Ive wanted to play it forever!

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Sat Feb 14, 2009 11:33 pm I wish these games would come to the USA but Bandai (I believe who owns the rights to the video game series) never wants to release them outside of Japan.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Feb 15, 2009 11:12 am Guyver Spawn wrote: I wish these games would come to the USA but Bandai (I believe who owns the rights to the video game series) never wants to release them outside of Japan.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Mon Feb 16, 2009 7:47 pm GAINAX just loves that cashcow. How is the special edition supposed to differ from the original release?

[]:             Quote Post by Lleonard Pler » Sun Feb 22, 2009 11:50 am I'll buy as a collector piece (like I did with Secret of Evangelion), but it's about time that they release a new Evangelion game for PSP. Or at least, release more interesting games (I mean, not text adventures) like Detective Evangelion or Battle Orchestra.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Feb 23, 2009 4:10 pm I'm pre-ordering the limited edition version. I'll take pictures of what I can and upload it on here once I get it. Lleonard Pler: Would you say Secret of Evangelion is a decent game (in general as well as for PSP)?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Apr 01, 2009 4:01 pm GoS 2 is getting the PSP treatment as well: http://www.evastore.jp/index.cgi?rm=detail&id=2513&genre=&page=&key=&8%A1%E04=2%A1%DE0
summary:
[]: [GAME] GoS Special Edition and GoS 2 released on PSP!             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Feb 13, 2009 7:52 pm Looks like Gainax beat Zeak to it: http://www.evageeks.org/2009/02/girlfriend-of-steel-to-be-released-on-sony-psp/ Discuss. Personally, I'm glad. Now I can see the changes between the Special edition compared to the regular version! (yay for having a PSP!)
